bullets.text: Progressive recorded record operating income of $485.5 million in 2001 with a 4.8% underwriting margin and a GAAP combined ratio of 95.5, recovering from the 2000 loss cycle; ROE was 13.5% versus only 1.7% in 2000.

Personal Lines earned premium grew 11% to $6.5 billion with a 4% increase in auto policies in force; Direct business earned premium surged 46% to $1.8 billion with 18% PIF growth; Agent business earned premium was $4.7 billion.

Agent channel policies in force declined 1% due to rate adequacy discipline; Direct channel policies in force grew 18%; Agent channel combined ratio was an exceptional 94.7; Direct channel combined ratio improved 12.8 points to 97.7.

Investment portfolio grew to $8.2 billion from $7.0 billion; $350 million debt issued in December 2001; repurchased 932,234 shares; total return on portfolio was 5.9% fully-taxable equivalent; asset allocation was 84% fixed income, 16% equities.

Progressive began testing end-to-end vehicle repair service centers offering drop-off, courtesy car, and guaranteed repairs; sent 4,100 proactive e-mails to policyholders after Tropical Storm Allison; settled 90% of 6,000+ claims within 50 days.

Progressive advanced paper-free agent relationships across multiple states; growing share of policy endorsements performed online by agents; Internet buying and servicing capabilities described as industry-leading.

2001 was the first full year under the reorganized structure separating Agent and Direct channels with distinct managers, objectives, and technology strategies; the structure produced enhanced focus and brand opportunities.

New CEO Glenn Renwick introduced the Customer Value Proposition ('Fast, Fair, Better') as a fourth pillar alongside Vision, Values, and Objectives; launched monthly public reporting of underwriting results, a first among public insurers.

Progressive's cycle management thesis played out as predicted: early rate increases ahead of competitors led to temporary growth slowdown while competitors deteriorated, positioning Progressive for profitable growth recovery in 2002.
